channel.title: My Home Lab Experiments on Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in My Home Lab Experiments on Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD

item.title: The posts I have never written
item.description: Every time I embark on a new exciting project, I plan to write about it. Sometimes life gets in and my to do list only increases. Here is an (incomplete) list of posts I have started, but never finished. If you are interested in any of the topics, please send me an email and I’ll make sure that it appears. By writing this list out I hope that I will be able to focus the most of my energy on a single project at a time.

item.title: Connecting To Raspberry Pi Over WiFi
item.description: Raspberry Pi is great. It is a small and cheap computer that can be used for a variety of projects. Sometimes you may wish to remotely control a Rasperry Pi that is in your local WiFi network. This is possible and even quite easy. We will use a Secure Shell aka SSH to establish a remote connection. I assume that you have previously connected your Raspberry Pi board to a WiFi network.
